title: The Star Garden
summary: Tom and Maya find a special garden in space that grows without land.
coverScene: Tom and Maya float near a tiny light garden inside a small ship, light stars around them.

sentences.text: Tom was a young man who always loved to look at the many stars from his small ship.
He often traveled alone but he always hoped to meet a very friend who shared his love of space.
One day he met Maya, a young scientist who was studying the very light from far stars.
They became friends and decided to explore together a region of space that few people had visited.
Their ship moved toward a white spot that seemed to show a very strange pattern.
When they approached, they saw a tiny garden float in space, still surrounded by a light blue halo.
The garden was found by Tom and Maya as they scanned the very area for any signs of life.
It was a small plot of green leaves that seemed to grow without any material, still thriving.
The leaves float and always produce tiny fruits that look like red balls.
Tom realized that the garden could provide food for their very long journey across the big space.
Maya, who studied many plants, explained that the garden used light and water to grow food without material, always efficiently.
They decided to take the garden aboard their ship and to care for it as a very tiny friend.
Back on the ship, they placed the garden in a light room where the light could show through the windows, very.
Each day they water the plants with a small amount of water from the ship tanks, always checking the level.
They also check the light levels and adjust the position of the garden to keep it in light, very precisely.
If the light became too weak, they would turn the screen to show more light onto the leaves, very urgently.
When the plants grew, they harvested the fruit and ate it together, feeling strong and happy, always grateful.
They wrote notes about the garden growth and saved the data for future explorers who might need a very similar garden.
One night, a system on the ship warned them of a very coming space storm that would pass close to their route.
The storm was described as a small but high wave of strong matter that could wash away loose objects, very dangerous.
If they ignored the warning, the storm might hit the ship and the garden could be lost, very soon.
If they had known the storm earlier, they would have prepared the garden by securing it in a very strong hold.
Maya suggested that they move the garden to the lower deck, where the ship could keep it even better.
Tom agreed and began to plan how to keep the garden safe during the storm, very seriously.
They locked the garden in a strong box and placed the box in the ship safe corner, very securely.
They also tied the box to the floor so that it would not move when the ship moved, very tightly.
If the storm became stronger, they would add extra hold to the box to keep it steady, very firmly.
They checked the water supply and made sure there was enough for the garden during the storm, very carefully.
Maya reminded Tom that the garden needed light, so they set a small light to show on the leaves, very.
They felt ready and hoped that the garden would stay safe during the storm and stay to grow, very hopeful.
When the storm arrived, the ship moved and the lights show as the wave of matter hit the ship, very.
The garden box moved, but the extra hold kept it from falling out of its place, very securely.
If the box had not been secured, the garden would have been lost in the storm, very dangerous.
The storm was strong, yet the ship kept most of the damage away from the crew, very effectively.
After several minutes, the wave passed and the ship returned to a still state, very still.
Tom and Maya checked the garden and were happy to see that the leaves were still green and the fruit red, very.
They realized that the garden had survived because they had prepared it well and had acted quickly, very grateful.
If they had not taken the time to secure the garden, the storm would have taken it away always, very sadly.
They wrote a report about the garden and the storm to help other people who might face a similar risk, very important.
Maya said that the garden could be a great help for future trips that need food without a long line, very useful.
Tom agreed and thought that the garden might become a key part of space travel for many people, very hopeful.
They looked at the stars, felt happy, and promised to keep the garden safe wherever they would go, very determined.
The tiny garden floated still in the ship room, a reminder that life can grow even in the big space, very inspiring.
They smiled, ready to face the next trip, knowing that the garden would always be their very star garden.
In the quiet of space, Tom and Maya looked forward to new worlds where the garden could help many people.
They planned to map several stars and to find places where the garden could be placed safe.
Their ship would travel far, and the garden would grow food for the crew without needing a long supply line.
If another storm appeared, they would use the same strong box and extra hold to keep the garden.
Maya believed that the tiny garden could become a sign of hope for all who travel the big space.
Together they promised to share their story, so that future explorers would know that life can grow even in the big space.

quiz.q: What does the garden provide for Tom and Maya?
How do Tom and Maya protect the garden from the storm?
options: They keep it in a strong box
They move it to a lower deck

quiz.q: What do Tom and Maya plan to do with the garden in the future?
options: Share the story with future people

When they came they saw a small garden in space.

The garden had green leaves that grew without ground.
The leaves made red fruit like a thing.
Tom realized the garden could give food for their long travel.
Maya explained the garden used light and water to grow food.
They decided to take the garden with them and treat it friend.
Back in their room they placed the garden near a window.
Each day they gave water from the store and checked the light.
They moved the garden to keep it in light when needed.
When light was weak they turned a screen to add more light.
They harvested red fruit and ate together, feeling strong and happy.
They wrote notes about the garden growth for future travel.
One night a system warned them about a coming space storm.

If they ignored the warning the garden could be lost.
Maya suggested moving the garden to a lower floor for safety.
Tom agreed and began to plan how to keep the garden safe.
They put the garden in a strong box and kept it safe.
They tied the box to the floor so it would not move.
They added extra hold to the box when storm might be stronger.
They checked the water supply and made sure there was enough.
Maya set a small light to shine on the leaves.
They felt ready and hoped the garden would stay safe.
The storm arrived and their travel shook as matter hit.
The garden box moved but the extra hold kept it steady.
If the box had not been held the garden would be lost.
The storm was strong but the people stayed safe.
After minutes the storm passed and everything became still.
Tom and Maya checked the garden and saw green leaves.
They also saw red fruit on the leaves.
They realized the garden survived because they prepared it well.
They wrote a report about the garden and storm for other explore.
Maya said the garden could help future trips that need food.
Tom thought the garden might become a key part of space travel.
They looked at stars, felt happy.
They said they will keep the garden safe.
The garden stayed in their room, a sign that life can grow.
They smiled, ready for the next trip.
They knew the garden will stay with them.
In quiet space they planned to map stars.
They wanted to find places for the garden.
Their travel will go far and the garden will grow food.
If another storm came they would use the same box and hold.
Maya believed the garden could be a sign of hope for travel.
Together they said they will share their story for future explore.
